title: When Forgiveness Was a Trap - Ada Palmer
summary: Dwarkesh Patel interviews historian Ada Palmer on the concept of forgiveness in historical contexts, examining when forgiveness functioned as a social trap or mechanism. The discussion explores philosophical and historical dimensions of forgiveness as a social practice.
writeup: Dwarkesh Patel interviews historian Ada Palmer about forgiveness as a historical and social practice, focusing on circumstances in which it operated as a trap rather than a release. The conversation traces philosophical and historical dimensions of how forgiveness functioned across past contexts. It matters because it reframes a familiar moral concept as a mechanism with social consequences that shifted by setting.
